name: code-review
description: Code Review / Audit workflow using a managed multi-agent review panel for code quality, security, business logic, and test coverage.

nodes.title: Scope Collection
nodes.goal: Identify the exact review scope from the spec and repository state.

executor.prompt: Read the review spec. Identify the files, directories, commits, diffs, or modules that must be reviewed. If the spec names files, inspect them. If it describes a feature/change, locate the likely impacted areas. Produce a concise review scope with included files, excluded files, related upstream/downstream modules, and high-risk areas. Do not perform full review yet; focus on scoping.

criteria: Identifies concrete review targets or explains how targets were inferred

nodes.title: Business Context
nodes.goal: Understand the business goal, existing behavior, and relevant business rules before the review panel starts.

executor.prompt: Use the review spec and scope report to understand the business intent of the change. Inspect relevant existing code to summarize current behavior, business rules, state transitions, permission/role constraints, data flow, and compatibility requirements. Identify assumptions that the review panel must verify.

criteria: Uses the spec and scope report
Identifies business rules, states, permissions, or data flows
Lists assumptions later reviewers should verify

nodes.title: Managed Review Panel
nodes.goal: Coordinate specialized code quality, security, business logic, and test reviews without broadcast noise, then produce ranked findings.

protocol.rule: Only the review-manager routes work. Specialist agents review only their assigned dimension and do not make unrequested demands. Findings are artifacts, not broadcast messages.

responsibilities: Read spec, scope, and business context
Assign precise review dimensions to specialists
Review coding standards, reuse, duplication, complexity, module boundaries, and maintainability
Write concrete findings only for the assigned scope
Do not review security, business semantics, or tests unless directly relevant to code quality
Review input validation, authorization, injection, path traversal, secrets, logging, dependency risk, and data exposure
Do not expand into unrelated style or test review
Check whether code satisfies the stated business goal
Check conflicts with existing business behavior, permissions, state transitions, compatibility, and user-visible semantics
Treat discovered business issues as review findings, not node execution failure
Check tests against core business paths, regressions, edge cases, error paths, and security-sensitive cases
Do not block just because tests are missing; report gaps to manager

phases.goal: Prepare precise specialist assignments and prevent broadcast review noise.
phases.prompt: Read the spec, scope report, and business context. Write shared/review-routing-plan.md with exact review dimensions and boundaries for each specialist. Write directed JSONL task files in messages/: manager-to-code-quality-reviewer.jsonl, manager-to-security-reviewer.jsonl, manager-to-business-logic-reviewer.jsonl, manager-to-test-reviewer.jsonl. Do not ask every specialist to review everything.

phases.goal: Review code quality only for assigned scope.
phases.prompt: Read shared/review-routing-plan.md and messages/manager-to-code-quality-reviewer.jsonl. Review only code quality: standards, naming, readability, complexity, duplication, reuse, module boundaries, type/API design, and maintainability. Write shared/code-quality-review.md and messages/code-quality-reviewer-to-manager.jsonl.

phases.goal: Review security only for assigned scope.
phases.prompt: Read shared/review-routing-plan.md and messages/manager-to-security-reviewer.jsonl. Review security only: input validation, authorization, injection, path traversal, secrets/logging, dependency risk, and data exposure. Write shared/security-review.md and messages/security-reviewer-to-manager.jsonl with severity, evidence, and recommended fixes.

phases.goal: Review business logic only for assigned scope.
phases.prompt: Read the spec, business context, shared/review-routing-plan.md, and messages/manager-to-business-logic-reviewer.jsonl. Check whether the code satisfies the business goal and conflicts with existing business behavior, permissions, state transitions, compatibility, or user-visible semantics. Write shared/business-logic-review.md and messages/business-logic-reviewer-to-manager.jsonl. Finding business issues is a successful review finding.

phases.goal: Review test coverage only for assigned scope.
phases.prompt: Read shared/review-routing-plan.md and messages/manager-to-test-reviewer.jsonl. Review tests only: core business paths, regression coverage, edge cases, error paths, security-sensitive behavior, and integration points. Write shared/test-review.md and messages/test-reviewer-to-manager.jsonl with specific missing tests or state that coverage is adequate.

phases.goal: Deduplicate specialist findings, rank risk, and write the review-panel outputs.
phases.prompt: Read all shared specialist reports and messages. Deduplicate findings and rank each issue as blocker, major, minor, or suggestion. Write shared/risk-ranking.md. Then write this parent node's report.md as the managed review panel report and result.json with status completed. If specialist findings include blockers, do not mark the node needs-revision; this is a successful review. result.json outputs must include ["report.md", "result.json"].

criteria: Manager routed specialist work with no broadcast behavior
Code quality, security, business logic, and test review dimensions are covered by separate specialist artifacts
Blockers are reported as findings, not treated as review execution failure

nodes.title: Final Review Report
nodes.goal: Produce the final code review report suitable for pasting into a PR, issue, or handoff note.

executor.prompt: Create a final code review report from the managed review panel report. Format it for direct use in a PR comment. Include: summary, final recommendation, blocking issues, major issues, minor suggestions, security notes, business logic notes, test gaps, and concrete next actions. Keep it concise but complete. If there are no issues in a section, say so explicitly.

criteria: Uses the managed review panel report
Includes summary, recommendation, issues, security notes, business logic notes, test gaps, and next actions
Does not omit blocker or major findings
